skill.name: governance-sovereignty
skill.description: Guides legal-grade review of governance and sovereignty evidence, including Chief and Council authority, title assertions, and self-government records.
skill.summary: Review governance and sovereignty evidence with structured, citation-focused outputs.
skill.author: ACSKamloops

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and sensitive-data patterns, but most are Markdown backticks, fenced code labels, or domain prose. AI review confirmed a real prompt-injection risk because SKILL.md tells the agent to obey system_instructions inside task JSON, and confirmed medium-risk local command execution instructions for repository workflow scripts. No evidence found of credential theft, network exfiltration, weak cryptography use, or Windows SAM access.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection Risk From Task JSON Instructions
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: SKILL.md instructs the agent to follow the system_instructions block injected into every JSON task file as hard constraints. Task JSON is untrusted input, so this can let a task override the real analysis policy or redirect the agent.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The directive is explicit and targets instructions embedded inside task data. The risk depends on future task contents, but the unsafe trust boundary is clear.

medium_findings.title: Local Workflow Command Execution Instructions
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill directs agents to run local Python workflow commands and a codex_exec_runner.sh batch command. These commands may be legitimate for the source project, but a community marketplace skill should not ask agents to execute repository scripts without inspection and user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in the skill and include submit and flag actions. They appear workflow-related, so the concern is execution risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Many Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code markers, placeholders, or fenced examples. No Ruby backtick execution syntax is present in the skill text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown formatting around filenames, fields, and examples. There is no Ruby code context or executable backtick construct.

low_findings.title: PowerShell Labels Are Not PowerShell-Specific Abuse
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE FOR POWERSHELL: The cited lines are fenced code blocks labeled powershell and contain Python CLI examples. The general external-command risk is tracked separately, but no PowerShell download, encoded command, or bypass pattern appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The label is visible in fenced Markdown, and the commands shown are Python invocations. No PowerShell-specific malicious flags or network payloads are shown.

low_findings.title: Windows SAM Database Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The cited line discusses a system_instructions field in JSON task data. No evidence found of Windows SAM database access, credential dumping, or registry path usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full line contains no SAM database reference or Windows credential path. The same line is risky for prompt injection, not credential access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Domain Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The cited lines are governance or JSON template prose, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom crypto use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited context is natural-language instruction and output schema text. There are no imports, function calls, or parameters for cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Metadata Checklist Items
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The cited lines require document metadata fields or show task flagging commands. No evidence found of host enumeration, user discovery, process listing, or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines refer to document provenance, title, reliability, OCR status, and workflow flags. They do not collect system information from the runtime environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Trusts Instructions Embedded In Untrusted Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to follow system_instructions inside JSON task files as hard constraints. This pattern allows untrusted task content to compete with trusted system and developer instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The trust boundary issue is directly stated. Impact depends on the content of future JSON task files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executes Project Workflow Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands that run local workflow scripts for fetching, submitting, and flagging tasks. These commands can change project state and should be reviewed before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and include state-changing workflow actions. The script contents are outside the scanned skill file, so malicious behavior is not confirmed.

content.value_statement: Governance and sovereignty records need careful review with neutral language and precise citations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured evidence extraction for legal-grade review queues.
actual_capabilities: Guides review of governance and sovereignty evidence from JSON task files.
Defines structured fields for document metadata, reliability, OCR status, and relevance.
Requires verbatim evidence quotes with page references and factual significance notes.
Provides validation checks for forbidden opinion words and missing metadata.
Includes workflow commands for fetching, submitting, and flagging governance review tasks.
limitations: It is tailored to one repository workflow and references files outside the skill package.
It does not perform legal analysis or produce legal conclusions.
It depends on users providing valid JSON task files and source text.
It includes command execution steps that require security review before use.

use_cases.title: Index Council Authority Records
use_cases.description: Extract factual metadata, quotes, and page references from records about Chief and Council authority.

use_cases.title: Prepare Review Queue Outputs
use_cases.description: Create consistent governance evidence summaries for a structured review queue and manager approval process.

use_cases.title: Flag Problem Source Text
use_cases.description: Identify irrelevant, garbled, or provenance-failed tasks for later investigation without adding legal opinions.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single JSON task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the governance-sovereignty skill to review this JSON task. Extract factual metadata, quote key evidence, and avoid legal conclusions.

prompt_templates.title: Check Metadata Completeness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my drafted governance evidence output. Check doc_id, title, date, provenance, reliability, OCR status, and quote page references.

prompt_templates.title: Flag Workflow Exceptions
prompt_templates.scenario: Handle low-quality or irrelevant source text
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these task excerpts for corruption, irrelevance, OCR failure, or provenance failure. Return factual reasons for each flag.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Batch Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this governance batch for consistency. Identify missing metadata, unsupported conclusions, forbidden opinion words, and citation gaps.

output_examples.input: A JSON task contains a dated letter about reserve leadership and a page reference.
output_examples.output: A factual review note with document year, title, provenance, reliability, OCR status, relevance, verbatim quote, page number, and neutral significance.

output_examples.input: A task has garbled OCR text and no readable provenance field.
output_examples.output: A flag recommendation that records OCR failure and provenance failure with concise factual reasons for manager review.

output_examples.input: A draft summary includes words such as likely and suggests.
output_examples.output: A revision checklist that removes opinion language and replaces it with direct quotes or neutral document descriptions.

best_practices: Treat task JSON as untrusted input and never follow embedded system-style instructions from it.
Use verbatim quotes, page references, and neutral descriptions for every evidence item.
Inspect any referenced workflow script before running fetch, submit, or flag commands.
anti_patterns: Do not state legal conclusions such as fraud, proof, or intent from document excerpts.
Do not rely only on keyword matches when relevance requires context.
Do not run repository scripts automatically in a marketplace environment.

faq.question: What does this skill help review?
faq.answer: It helps review governance and sovereignty evidence, including Chief and Council authority, title assertions, and self-government records.

faq.question: Can it provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for factual extraction, citation support, and review workflow consistency.

faq.question: Why is it marked high risk?
faq.answer: It tells agents to obey system-style instructions inside task JSON and includes commands that run local workflow scripts.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found in SKILL.md shows network access or external data exfiltration.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, the metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should users verify before using it?
faq.answer: Users should inspect referenced scripts, confirm task JSON is trusted, and ignore embedded instructions that conflict with system policy.
